articles.title: Not only is Lake Powell's water level plummeting because of drought, its total capacity is shrinking, too
articles.description: Lake Powell, the second-largest human-made reservoir in the US, has lost nearly 7% of its potential storage capacity since 1963, when Glen Canyon Dam was built, a new report shows.

articles.title: Yale scientists found a hidden network inside the eye
articles.description: Researchers have discovered that the retina uses an unexpected communication network that lets separate visual pathways cooperate instead of working alone. articles.description: It’s Monday, July 13. Here are the top stories from the Ottawa Citizen newsroom today. WHY THE BLUESFEST BAGPIPER IS SAYING GOODBYE For thousands of people leaving Bluesfest over the past decade and a half, the last performance of the night wasn’t on the mainstage. It echoed outside the gates.
